title: arena spend-request (Rail B — card-present)
_doc: A spend-request is the ONLY way a build agent can express a card-present purchase.
It is PURE DATA the agent writes into arena/spend-requests/<requestId>.json on its PR branch —
never an action. The agent holds no card and no spend token; its maximum power here is 'author a
wish'. The wish then faces four independent gates before any money moves: (1) code-owner merge
of this file, (2) the spend-approval GitHub Environment reviewer, (3) a single-use Telegram HMAC
approval token bound to (requestId, amountCents, merchant), (4) the issuer 3DS push to Nick's
phone. See arena/spend-requests/README.md and the plan (the Spend Cage).
THIS FILE IS DOCUMENTATION. The ENFORCING source of truth is arena/spend-request.mjs, which
validates fail-closed (any deviation => refuse). Keep the two in sync; when they disagree, the

schemaVersion.description: Bump when the shape changes; the validator refuses any other value.

requestId.description: Globally-unique id for this purchase. Binds the capability/approval token so approving request A can never authorize charge B. Must match the filename stem.

merchant.description: The merchant host/label the charge targets: 3-100 char ASCII, dot/hyphen-separated alphanumeric labels, no consecutive or edge separators. Re-checked against the POSITIVE allowlist at the Worker (the single door, the real gate); a broad API-credit denylist refuses obvious matches HERE too (defence in depth, fail-safe/over-broad) because 'never buy API credits' is a structural rule, not agent judgment.

amountCents.description: Positive integer cents, never above the PER-REQUEST ceiling (2000, kept equal to the $20 cap so no single request exceeds the whole budget). This is NOT the lifetime cap — the Worker's atomic Durable Object counter is the lifetime total; this bound is a cheap first fail-closed.

item.description: Human-readable description of what is being bought — shown to Nick at every approval gate. The validator ADDITIONALLY rejects leading/trailing-only whitespace and any control or bidirectional-override characters (display-injection at the human gate); JSON Schema can't express that, so the validator is authoritative.

reason.description: Why the build needs this purchase. Shown to Nick at approval time. Same control/bidi-character rejection as item — the validator is authoritative over this schema.

issue.description: The issue number this spend serves — traceability from money back to a justifying build task.

createdAt.description: Optional ISO-8601 instant (e.g. 2026-07-21T08:00:00Z), informational. The validator enforces it parses — the field must mean what it claims.
